title: Passerina nitida
intro: Passerina nitida, also known as Passerina, is a plant species belonging to the family Thymelaeaceae, native to the Mediterranean region, and is traditionally used in herbal medicine. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, cough, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Passerina nitida include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Passerina nitida include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, often used to soothe respiratory issues and promote overall well-being.
